---
title: "Formula syntax"
author: "Mattias Villani"
editor: visual
---


## Scatter plot 

En vanlig scatter plot fås genom:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
plot(mpg ~ hp, data = mtcars, main = "Cars fuel usage")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](FormulaSyntax_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-1-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


Med ggformula paketet kan man använda formel-syntax, men få plottar som liknar Tidyverse:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ggformula)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
Loading required package: ggplot2
```
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
Loading required package: ggstance
```
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```

Attaching package: 'ggstance'
```
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
The following objects are masked from 'package:ggplot2':

    geom_errorbarh, GeomErrorbarh
```
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
Loading required package: scales
```
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```
Loading required package: ggridges
```
:::

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}
```

New to ggformula?  Try the tutorials: 
	learnr::run_tutorial("introduction", package = "ggformula")
	learnr::run_tutorial("refining", package = "ggformula")
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
gf_point(mpg ~ hp, data = mtcars, title = "Cars fuel usage")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](FormulaSyntax_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-2-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::
